title: cyathea eggersii
intro: Cyathea eggersii, also known as the Eggers' tree fern, is a species of tree fern that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The leaves of the Cyathea eggersii plant can be used to make teas and infusions that may help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and support the immune system.
